statement.markdown:
You are given the head of a linked list and an integer k. The task is to reverse the linked list and remove the first k nodes.

The input is a head of a linked list and an integer k. The output should be the head of the modified linked list.

Example: If the input linked list is 1 -> 2 -> 3 -> 4 -> 5 and k = 2, the output linked list should be 4 -> 3 -> 2 -> 1.

Note: The input linked list is 1-indexed.
statement.inputFormat: The first line of input contains an integer k. The next line contains the number of nodes in the linked list, followed by an array of integers, each representing a node value.
statement.outputFormat: The output is the head of the modified linked list.

constraints.description: The number of nodes to be removed from the beginning of the linked list.

examples.explanation: Reverse the linked list and remove the first 2 nodes.
Reverse the linked list and remove the first 3 nodes.

hints.text: You can solve this problem by first reversing the linked list, and then removing the first k nodes.

editorial.title: Correct Approach to Reverse Linked List
editorial.intuition: To reverse the linked list, you can use a recursive function or a while loop. Then, you can remove the first k nodes by updating the head of the linked list.
editorial.bruteForce: You can use a recursive function to reverse the linked list, but this approach will have a high time complexity.
editorial.betterSolution: You can use a while loop to reverse the linked list, which will have a lower time complexity.
editorial.optimalSolution: To remove the first k nodes, you can update the head of the linked list by moving the head pointer k nodes.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: You can use a dry run to test your solution and ensure it is correct.
commonMistakes: Not properly reversing the linked list
Not removing the first k nodes

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: ListNode* solve(ListNode* head, int k){ if (k == 0) return head; ListNode* newHead = head->next; head->next = solve(newHead, k-1); return newHead; } };
buggyStarterCode.java: class Solution { public ListNode solve(ListNode head, int k){ if (k == 0) return head; ListNode newHead = head.next; head.next = solve(newHead, k-1); return newHead; } }

buggyStarterCode.javascript: function solve(head, k) { if (k == 0) return head; var newHead = head.next; head.next = solve(newHead, k-1); return newHead; }
